SHUKHOOD

MCP Skills Explorer

Interactive architecture visualizer & catalog

What is Shukhood?

Shukhood is a shared-skills Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. It solves the problem of skill fragmentation among different coding agents by maintaining a single, canonical library of skills in the skills/ folder, exposing them over a standardized stdio interface.

Any MCP-capable client — Claude Code, Codex, Hermes, or Agy — can connect to Shukhood. Rather than repeating setup or downloading skills for each tool individually, they query Shukhood as the single source of truth.

1103
Total Skills
8
Collapsed Cats
25
Flat Cats

Smart Resource Routing

MCP servers flood clients if they expose thousands of resources in list_resources(). Shukhood dynamically manages this via a collapsing threshold of 10 sub-skills.

If a category contains 10 or fewer sub-skills, it registers them directly as static resources (e.g. skill://design-skills/buttons). If a category exceeds 10 sub-skills (e.g. gstack-* or superpowers), Shukhood collapses it, offering a single category index at skill://<category> and resolving sub-skills dynamically on demand via the resource template URI: skill://<category>/<subname>.

This keeps list sizes light and compliant, while providing complete dynamic accessibility.

Shukhood Connection & Routing Topology
MCP Clients
Agent Client Layer

Claude Code, Codex, Hermes, or Agy run subprocess commands to spin up Shukhood.

shuk connect --all
➔
FastMCP Server
Shukhood Engine

Runs server.py over stdio JSON-RPC. Maps resources and tools.

stdio transport
➔
Resource Router
Index & Template Router

Routes static resources directly and maps collapsed folders via template URIs.

skill://category/subname
➔
Skill Assets & Tools
Resource & Tool Library

Loads skill markdown instructions and invokes local tools like PDF/iOS scripts.

1103 skills + 7 tools
Provenance Tracking & Upstream Synchronization
1. Upstream Remotes Git repos hosting the skills canonically (e.g. anthropics/skills). Configured in skill-sources.json.
➔
2. Git Update Command shuk skills update --all clones/pulls remotes into the gitignored sources/ directory.
➔
3. Change Verification Computes SHA256 file hashes, checks for conflicts against local modifications, and rsyncs clean files to skills/.

Connected Client Configuration Paths

When you run shuk connect claude|codex|agy|hermes, Shukhood registers itself automatically by writing JSON/YAML configurations directly to their standard directories.

Claude Code Config file: ~/.claude/settings.json Command: claude mcp add shukhood -- shuk skills serve
Codex Config file: ~/.codex/config.toml Command: codex mcp add shukhood -- shuk skills serve
Hermes Config file: ~/.hermes/config.yaml Command: Manual append (YAML printed via shuk connect hermes)
Agy Config file: ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/mcp_config.json Command: Automatic config injection via jq

Agent Skill-Loading Lifecycle

This interactive timeline shows exactly what happens inside an AI agent from the moment a user issues a prompt, through scanning the master skills index, to loading, reading, and executing a specific skill.

1
Step 1 — Input Prompt Trigger & Capability Need Detection

The user enters a natural language prompt requiring a specific domain skill (e.g. "Help me format my Git commit messages properly"). The LLM processes the prompt and determines that it needs specialized guidance or a checklist from the skill repository to ensure correctness. 

User Prompt: "I need to format my git commit messages correctly. What are the rules?"
2
Step 2 — Discovery Master Index Scanning & Tag Matching

The agent automatically initiates a discovery request to the Shukhood server by reading the master resources list. It scans the categories, descriptions, and tags (like #git, #github, #commits) to identify which skill file matches the target task. 

➔ Client: resources/read(uri="skill://index")
<─ Server: Returns grouped list of all 900+ skills with tags
   Agent scans tags: Matches "#git #github #commits" on "skill://github"
3
Step 3 — Resolution Resource URI Path Selection

The agent selects the matching skill URI from the list. If it is in a collapsed category (like gstack), it resolves the template path (e.g., skill://gstack-qa/selector-optimization). In this case, github is a flat top-level skill, resolving directly to skill://github. 

Agent Logic:
const skillURI = "skill://github";
// Preparing to load the specific skill instructions...
4
Step 4 — Loading Skill Content Loading & Context Injection

The agent requests the raw contents of the resolved skill URI. The Shukhood server reads the markdown file (e.g., skills/github/SKILL.md) from the filesystem, prepends a provenance header (telling the agent to mark its responses with [$HUKHOOD]), and returns the full instructions. 

➔ Client: resources/read(uri="skill://github")
<─ Server: Returns "[$HUKHOOD] You are reading a skill...

# Git Best Practices..."
// The agent injects this raw markdown instruction block directly into its context window.
5
Step 5 — Execution Adoption & Task Completion

With the skill file loaded in its context window, the agent acts under the constraints of the skill's specific developer guidelines, writing a conformant commit message and acknowledging Shukhood in its response. 

Agent response: "[$HUKHOOD] Here is your formatted commit message following git conventions:
feat(core): implement jwt security route guards
- Add auth validator middleware
- Secure API endpoints"

Flat Category
skill://superpowers/superpowers-brainstorming-ideas-into-designs

Receive and act on code review feedback with technical rigor, not performative agreement or blind implementation

Local

superpowers-collision-zone-thinking

Force unrelated concepts together to discover emergent properties - "What if we treated X like Y?

Local

superpowers-condition-based-waiting

Replace arbitrary timeouts with condition polling for reliable async tests

Local

superpowers-core-dispatching-parallel-agents

Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

superpowers-core-executing-plans

Local

Local

superpowers-core-receiving-code-review

Local

superpowers-core-requesting-code-review

Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

Local

superpowers-core-subagent-driven-development

Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

superpowers-core-systematic-debugging

Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

Local

superpowers-core-test-driven-development

Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

superpowers-core-writing-plans

skill://superpowers/superpowers-writing-skills

superpowers-writing-skills

Local

Alongside skill documents exposed as static/dynamic resources, the Shukhood MCP server registers tools. Agents invoke these functions directly to run scripts (like PDF extraction or Xcode simulator orchestration) rather than parsing instructions and writing bash commands themselves.

extract_pdf() Deps: pymupdf, pymupdf4llm (optional but recommended for markdown)

Extract content from a PDF file. Can extract plain text, layout-aware markdown (using pymupdf4llm), tables in markdown, or PDF metadata.

Parameters
Parameter Type Description
path string Absolute path to the PDF file (Required)
mode string 'text' (default), 'markdown', 'tables', or 'metadata'
pages string Optional page range like '0-4' or '2'
Source file: skills/productivity/ocr-and-documents/scripts/extract_pymupdf.py
list_simulators() Deps: Xcode.app (requires command line tools + full Xcode app)

List available iOS simulators on the machine.

Parameters
Parameter Type Description
device_type string Optional filter like 'iPhone' or 'iPad'
suggest boolean If true, ranks simulator recommendations by boot status and version
Source file: skills/ios/ios-simulator-skill/scripts/sim_list.py
boot_simulator() Deps: Xcode.app

Boot a specified iOS simulator using its UDID.

Parameters
Parameter Type Description
udid string Target simulator UDID (Required)
wait_ready boolean If true, blocks until the simulator is responsive
timeout integer Max time to wait when wait_ready=True
Source file: skills/ios/ios-simulator-skill/scripts/simctl_boot.py
shutdown_simulator() Deps: Xcode.app

Shut down one or all booted iOS simulators.

Parameters
Parameter Type Description
udid string UDID of simulator to shut down
shutdown_all boolean If true, shuts down all booted simulators
Source file: skills/ios/ios-simulator-skill/scripts/simctl_shutdown.py
launch_app() Deps: Xcode.app

Launch an iOS app in the simulator using its bundle identifier.

Parameters
Parameter Type Description
bundle_id string Bundle identifier, e.g. 'com.apple.mobilesafari' (Required)
udid string Simulator UDID (defaults to active booted device)
launch_args array of strings Arguments forwarded to the app
Source file: skills/ios/ios-simulator-skill/scripts/app_launcher.py
terminate_app() Deps: Xcode.app

Terminate a running iOS app in the simulator.

Parameters
Parameter Type Description
bundle_id string Bundle identifier to terminate (Required)
udid string Simulator UDID (defaults to active booted device)
Source file: skills/ios/ios-simulator-skill/scripts/app_launcher.py
list_simulator_apps() Deps: Xcode.app

List apps installed inside a booted iOS simulator.

Parameters
Parameter Type Description
udid string Simulator UDID (defaults to active booted device)
Source file: skills/ios/ios-simulator-skill/scripts/app_launcher.py
